questions.question: Define a union in C and explain how memory is allocated for union members.
keyPoints: Union is a user-defined data type that stores different data types in the same memory location
All members share the same memory space
Memory allocated equals the size of the largest member only
Unlike structure which allocates separate memory for each member

questions.question: Differentiate between structure and union with respect to memory allocation.
keyPoints: Structure allocates separate memory for each member
Union allocates shared memory for all members
Size of structure = sum of all members (with padding)
Size of union = size of largest member only
In union, modifying one member affects all others

questions.question: Write a C program to demonstrate how the same memory location can store different data types using union.
keyPoints: Declare a union with int and float members
Assign value to int member
Display value using both int and float interpretation
Explain that same bits are interpreted differently
Shows memory sharing concept

questions.question: What is an anonymous union? Write a C code snippet demonstrating its usage inside a structure.
keyPoints: Anonymous union has no name
Declared directly inside another structure
Members accessed directly without union name
Syntax: struct Example { union { int x; char y; }; };
No need for dot operator to access union members

questions.question: Explain the concept of tagged union and why it is used.
keyPoints: Tagged union combines union with a type indicator (usually enum)
Enum field indicates which member is currently active
Prevents reading wrong interpretation of memory
Provides type safety
Common in implementing variant data types

questions.question: Write a C program to extract individual bytes from an integer using union.
keyPoints: Create union with int and char array[4]
Assign integer value to int member
Access each byte through array index
Useful for understanding byte-level storage
Demonstrates little-endian vs big-endian concepts

questions.question: Explain union in detail with suitable examples. Also write a C program to create a union to store employee information where an employee can have either a numeric ID (integer) or an alphanumeric code (character array), and demonstrate how to use it.
keyPoints: Define union with multiple member types
Explain memory sharing concept
Show size comparison with structure
Create employee union with int emp_id and char code[10]
Use type indicator to track which field is active
Display values properly based on active member
Emphasize memory efficiency

questions.question: Write a comprehensive C program that demonstrates the difference between structure and union. Your program should: (a) Create a structure and union with same members, (b) Display their sizes, (c) Show how values of one member affect others in union, (d) Explain the output.
keyPoints: Define structure and union with int, float, char members
Print sizeof for both
Initialize structure members and display all
Initialize union one member and display others
Show value corruption in union
Explain why this happens
Conclude with use cases for each

questions.question: Explain the practical applications of unions in C programming with code examples. Include examples for: (a) Memory-efficient storage, (b) Creating variant record types, and (c) Type punning for byte-level access.
keyPoints: Memory-efficient storage: use union to store either int/float/char
Variant record: use union with enum to track type
Type punning: use union to interpret memory as different types
Example programs for each scenario
Explain advantages and precautions
Discuss endianness considerations
Mention tagged union concept

questions.question: Write a C program to implement a simple calculator using union to store different types of operands (integer, float, character). Use appropriate type indicator to determine which operand type was used and display results accordingly.
keyPoints: Define enum for operand type
Create struct with union operand and enum type field
Functions to set int/float/char operands
Perform calculations based on type indicator
Display results with appropriate formatting
Demonstrates practical tagged union usage
Shows memory efficiency compared to separate fields
